fixtures.program: (,/) over n                   / ,/ is variadic and infix
16 +/ 1 2 3 4     / sum the list with starting value 16
g:+/[100;]       / 2-item argument list resolves the binary form
t:([]a:til 2;b:2#enlist (til 1;10))  / bad table, b is unmappable
t1:([]a:til 2;b:2#til 1)  / good table, b is mappable
helper:{$[(type x)or not count x;1;t:type first x;all t=type each x;0]};
select from (raze {([]table:enlist x;columns:enlist where not helper each flip .Q.en[`:.]`. x)} each tables[]) where 0<count each columns
sym                                     / contents of in-memory sym populated from symbols in table
get `:/tmp/db/sym                       / on-disk sym same as in-memory sym
get `:/tmp/db/t1/col1                   / col1 enumerated against sym domain
`:/tmp/db/t2/ set .Q.en[`:/tmp/db;t2];  / enumerate additional table against existing sym domain
sym                                     / in-memory sym now contains additional symbols
sp 2 3           / index simple table with integer list argument
select from s where i in 2 3
vec:100000 ? 30     / long vector with few different values
f:{exp x*x}         / e raised to x*x
a:til 10000000      / create an object that is ≥64MB
.Q.w[]              / view current heap size and how many bytes used of the heap (all objects plus previously allocated object)
.Q.gc[]             / garbage collection doesnt return any memory to OS
delete a from `.    / delete the original object, placing it on the heap
.Q.w[]              / used memory has decreased, heap remains the same
.Q.gc[]             / garbage collection has returned 134217728 to the OS from the heap
.Q.w[]              / heap size has reduced, while used memory remains the same
v:{(10#"a";10000#"b")}each til 1000000;   / create 1000000 rows, each containing 2 elements of 10 chars and 10000 chars
.Q.w[]              / used memory remains the same as before, but heap has reduced
.Q.w[]                                    / both heap and used memory has grown
.Q.gc[]             / garbage collection has found no slab of contiguous unused memory of ≥64MB to free
v:v[;0]             / change v to 1000000 rows, each only containing the 1st element of 10 chars (2nd element removed)
.Q.gc[]             / garbage collection has found no contiguous unused memory of ≥64MB to free
v:-8!v              / convert v into its serialised form, return vector used by v to heap
.Q.gc[]             / garbage collection now found unused contiguous memory slab ≥64MB to return to OS
v:-9!v              / convert serialised form of v back to its original state
t:select count i by date from trade
count .Q.ind[trade;(exec first sum x from t where date<2010.01.07)+til first exec x from t where date=2010.01.07]
(select from trade where date=2010.01.07)~.Q.ind[trade;(exec first sum x from t where date<2010.01.07)+til first exec x from t where date=2010.01.07]
.Q.li[2001.01.02 2001.01.03];select count i by date from t
/ print the formatted backtrace and error string to stderr
h:hopen`::5001   / f is defined on the remote
/ a made up protocol: (0;result) or (1;backtrace string)
1@(h"f `a")1;    / output the backtrace string to stdout
/open connections to 4 processes on the localhost
.z.pd:{n:abs system"s";$[n=count handles;handles;[hclose each handles;:handles::`u#hopen each 20000+til n]]}
/ argument x is the timestamp scheduled for the callback
/ .z.ts is called once per second and returns the timestamp
h".z.u"               / server side .z.u is as passed by the client to hopen
h({.z.w".z.u"};::)    / client side returns null symbol
`:fileA set til 1000    / create file, now compressed as .z.zd set
`:fileB set til 1000    / create file, now compressed as .z.zd set
`:file.something til 1000               / create file with extension while .z.zd set
-21!`:file.something                    / file not compressed, no compression info
(`:file.something;17;2;4) set til 1000  / create file with extension, pass compression settings on creation
show dict:``a`b!(17 5 3;17 2 6;17 2 6)  / default compression is `zstd` with level 3
